customManagers.description: Update Makefile tool versions via inline renovate comments

Track the .nvmrc Node version via the DOCKER datasource, querying the SAME `node:<ver>-alpine` tag variant the Dockerfile FROM line uses (via currentValueTemplate appending `-alpine`), and writing back only the bare version (autoReplaceStringTemplate `{{{newVersion}}}`, no `-alpine`, no digest) so mise/corepack/nvm can read it. Querying the identical alpine tag set is load-bearing: the plain `node:<ver>` (Debian) tags are rebuilt/re-pushed more often than the `-alpine` tags, so a docker-datasource lookup against the plain tags assigns a NEWER release timestamp to the same Node version — which makes minimumReleaseAge gate the two pins to DIFFERENT versions (.nvmrc lagging the Dockerfile by a release). Matching the alpine variant gives both pins identical timestamps, so they always resolve to the same version. The built-in `nvm` manager (node-version datasource, follows nodejs.org) is disabled in enabledManagers precisely because it cannot share Docker Hub's tag timeline.

Keep the `Node.js <ver>` version shown in the README Tech Stack table in lockstep with .nvmrc + the Dockerfile, so Renovate bumps the doc prose in the SAME 'Node toolchain' PR instead of leaving it to a manual re-sync. Uses the identical docker-datasource + alpine-variant + bare-writeback pattern as the .nvmrc manager (so it resolves to the same version with the same 3-day buffer). Grouped via matchDepNames=node; pinDigests disabled below so no @sha256 is appended to the prose.

packageRules.description: Wait 3 days before automerging major updates

Pin the undici override to the 7.x line. The override (pnpm-workspace.yaml) clears CVEs in undici 7.x, which is the line jsdom 29.1.1 hard-requires (`undici/lib/handler/wrap-handler.js`); undici v8 moved that path and breaks the vitest/jsdom test runtime. Remove this cap when jsdom ships a release that consumes undici >= 8.
Group GitHub Actions into one PR

Group the Node toolchain (.nvmrc via its custom manager + Dockerfile builder) so both bump together and stay aligned (enforced by `make check-node-alignment`). Two coupled root-cause fixes here: (1) both pins resolve from the DOCKER datasource (see the .nvmrc customManager), so the grouped PR proposes one identical version and they can no longer drift apart — matched by depName across managers, not matchManagers (the .nvmrc pin is a custom.regex manager now). (2) minimumReleaseAge holds the bump 3 days because a brand-new Node release's `node:<ver>-alpine` tag appears on Docker Hub as a multi-arch INDEX before its per-platform (amd64/arm64) child manifests finish propagating — building against it in that window fails the docker job with `no match for platform in manifest`. The 3-day buffer lets Docker Hub finish publishing before the PR opens. Node is build-time-only here (multi-stage; the runtime image is nginx), so delaying Node patches has no production-runtime security exposure.

Group Makefile tool versions. Scoped by file (matchFileNames: Makefile) rather than matchManagers: custom.regex, because the .nvmrc node pin is now ALSO a custom.regex manager — a matchManagers scope would wrongly fold it into this group.

Disable digest pinning for the Makefile docker-datasource dev tool (mermaid-cli). config:best-practices' docker:pinDigests otherwise generates a pinDigest update that collides with the version bump in the Makefile-tools branch and silently drops the bump. Scoped by file+datasource (NOT matchManagers, which surfaces as `regex` and can void the AND-combined rule). mermaid-cli is a lint tool, not production runtime; production digests (Dockerfile FROM) are untouched.

The .nvmrc node pin AND the README `Node.js <ver>` prose both resolve from the docker datasource (to stay in lockstep with the Dockerfile FROM pin), but must remain BARE version strings — .nvmrc is read by mise/corepack/nvm, the README is human prose. Disable config:best-practices' docker:pinDigests for them so Renovate never appends @sha256 and corrupts the file.

Hold mise tool bumps (act/hadolint/trivy/gitleaks/container-structure-test) for a 3-day stability buffer. mise's aqua: backend installs from GitHub Release assets but Renovate tracks them by tag (github-tags) — upstreams that push a tag before publishing the release binary 404 `mise install` (trivy is a recurring offender). The buffer lets the release publish before the PR opens.

Throttle the local Renovate CLI pin (RENOVATE_VERSION in the Makefile, custom.regex manager). Renovate publishes to npm several times a day; the npm manager's minimumReleaseAge does NOT cover a custom.regex pin, so this self-bump generated near-daily PR noise. matchDepNames matches regardless of manager. 7 days keeps this dev-only tool roughly weekly.
